content.assessment: From a public finance perspective, this proposal most directly targets the evidentiary gap that undermines value-for-money assessment across all other proposals: contested and unverified consolidation-savings claims (F-live-education_finance-02), routinely omitted transportation cost offsets (F-live-education_finance-03), and mixed evidence on closure's demographic/fiscal knock-on effects (F-live-education_finance-04). A registry tracking realized rather than projected savings would substantially improve the evidence base for future cost-incidence and value-for-money judgments, addressing a documented gap where past policy cycles were driven by political decision rather than verified fiscal outcomes. The chief limitation is that transparency infrastructure carries its own setup and maintenance costs and does not itself resolve fiscal magnitude or affordability questions, only informs them.
opportunities: Could substantially improve future value-for-money assessments for all other proposals (T1-T5) by supplying verified, comparable cost data currently absent from the corpb
strengths: Directly targets the contested/unverified nature of consolidation cost-savings claims (F-live-education_finance-02) by requiring tracking of realized, not projected, savings
Mandates inclusion of transportation cost offsets in cost accounting (F-live-education_finance-03), closing a documented gap in current closure-proposal cost visibility
Establishes a Hungary-specific evidence base rather than assuming international findings on closure/depopulation linkage transfer directly (F-live-education_finance-04)
threats: Historical pattern of top-down political decisions overriding technical evidence suggests an information-only framework may not change fiscal outcomes in practice
Delay in establishing infrastructure means demographic and fiscal pressures continue accumulating during the buildout period
weaknesses: An information/evidence infrastructure itself requires resources to build and maintain, adding to overall system cost without guaranteeing behavioral change
Does not resolve the underlying fiscal constraint of low per-pupil spending (F-live-education_finance-07); better information does not expand the resource envelope
